questions.scenario: An alert mentions `orders`, and the engineer finds a Cloud Run service with a similar name in `devpolaris-orders-staging`. Production is `devpolaris-orders-prod`. What should happen before any change?

options.text: Confirm the project ID, resource type, location, and exact resource path for the production target.
Change the staging service because the name is close enough.
Ignore the project because names are globally unique for every GCP service.
Change the billing account, because billing account names identify Cloud Run services.

questions.explanation: A friendly name is not enough. Project, type, location, and strong identifier make the target exact.

questions.scenario: A service account email is `orders-api-prod@devpolaris-orders-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com`. What does the email reveal immediately?

options.text: The service account's home project ID is visible in the email.
The service account has Owner on every project.
The Cloud Run service must be global.

questions.explanation: Service account emails include the project ID. Permissions still need IAM evidence, but the home project is visible.

questions.scenario: Which fields make a Cloud Run service target safer to review?

options.text: Only the word `orders` from a search result.

questions.explanation: The target should be exact enough for another engineer to find the same resource. A loose search word is not enough.
